title: hieracium doronicifolium subsp. neurophyllum
intro: Hieracium doronicifolium subsp. neurophyllum, commonly known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family, known for its yellow dandelion-like flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hieracium doronicifolium subsp. neurophyllum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
